title: rhododendron indicum var. sublanceolatum
intro: Rhododendron indicum var. sublanceolatum, also known as the Indian Rhododendron, is a shrub species native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rhododendron indicum var. sublanceolatum include its potential to help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
